I used conservation of energy: mgh = 1/2 m v^2, so v = sqrt(2gh), which matches choice C.
Only choice C has units of momentum (kg m/s), so by dimensional analysis it must be C.
The choices span many orders of magnitude, so I did a rough estimate and got ~10^3, which is D.
At the highest point the velocity is zero so drag vanishes and a = -g, answer B.
I checked the limit as the mass goes to infinity; only A survives, so A.
